etymology.description: 斤 (axe) + 廾 (two hands) = hands holding a weapon -> soldier; military
etymology.interpretation: The character 兵 (bīng, "soldier; military") is composed of 斤 (jīn, "a short axe"), representing a weapon, and 廾 (gǒng, "two hands"), depicting a pair of hands gripping the weapon. This combination intuitively symbolizes a person equipped with a weapon, directly leading to the meanings of "soldier" and "military."

examples.chinese: 他的梦想是当一名优秀的兵(他的夢想是當一名優秀的兵)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de mèngxiǎng shì dāng yī míng yōuxiù de bīng.
examples.english: His dream is to become an excellent soldier.

examples.chinese: 兵营里的生活很有纪律(兵營裡的生活很有紀律)。
examples.pinyin: Bīngyíng lǐ de shēnghuó hěn yǒu jìlǜ.
examples.english: Life in the barracks is very disciplined.

examples.chinese: 军队集结完毕(軍隊集結完畢)，准备出发(準備出發)。
examples.english: The troops have assembled and are ready to depart.
